   176→[abstract]
   177→.Abstract
   178→The Recipe Bottle Makefile Service enables safe and effective use of foreign
   179→{st_container_s}
   180→at the
   181→{at_operator_p}
   182→{at_workstation}.
   183→
   184→== System Overview 
   185→
   186→=== Core Trust Challenge
   187→
   188→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   189→
   190→{st_container}-
   191→based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   192→
   193→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   194→
   195→* {st_container_s}
   196→provide powerful tool isolation
   197→* But they need controlled access
   198→to do useful work  
   199→* Standard
   200→{st_container}
   201→networking
   202→grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   203→
   204→=== The Bottle Pattern
   205→
   206→{at_rbm_system}
   207→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   208→{st_container}
   209→type here referred to as a
   210→{at_bottle_container}:
   211→
   212→. File Access Boundary
   213→* {at_bottle_container}
   214→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   215→{st_volume_mount_s}
   216→* No other access
   217→to
   218→{at_workstation}
   219→filesystem
   220→
   221→. Network Boundary  
   222→* {at_bottle_container}
   223→has no direct network access
   224→* All traffic
   225→flows through dedicated
   226→{at_sentry_container},
   227→a
   228→{st_container}
   229→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   230→* Only declared
   231→{st_port_map_s}
   232→and destinations
   233→allowed
   234→* Full logging
   235→of allowed and denied traffic
   236→
   237→Each
   238→{at_bottle_service}
   239→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   240→
   241→* One
   242→{at_sentry_container}
   243→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   244→* One
   245→{at_censer_container}
   246→establishing and configuring the network namespace
   247→* One
   248→{at_bottle_container}
   249→running the actual service, sharing the censer's network namespace
   250→* Clear configuration
   251→in a single
   252→{at_nameplate}
   253→file
   254→
   255→The
   256→{at_censer_container}
   257→is a privileged container that establishes the network namespace, configures routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
   258→{at_sentry_container},
   259→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
   260→{at_bottle_container}
   261→via `--net=container:censer`.
   262→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet, and the
   263→{at_bottle_container}
   264→experiences only a functional path to its
   265→{at_sentry_container}
   266→gateway
   267→
   268→=== Security Properties
   269→
   270→The
   271→{at_rbm_system}
   272→maintains following essential guarantees:
   273→
   274→. File Access Control
   275→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   276→can access only explicitly configured
   277→{st_volume_mount_s}
   278→* No access to broader system resources
   279→
   280→. Network Isolation 
   281→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   282→{at_sentry_container}
   283→* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
   284→* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
   285→* Only explicitly configured
   286→{st_port_map_s}
   287→allow traffic between
   288→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network}
   289→* {at_bottle_container_s}
   290→have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
   291→{at_sentry_container_s}
   292→* Comprehensive traffic logging
   293→
   294→. Service Separation
   295→* Each
   296→{at_bottle_service}
   297→fully isolated
   298→* No cross-service communication
   299→* Independent security policies
   300→* Clean state separation
   301→
   302→// TODO fixup this language realized missing during pod namespace try
   303→. Service Access Control
   304→* External access to services only permitted when {rbrn_entry_mode} is enabled
   305→* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
   306→* All service access must originate from outside the pod
   307→* Entry traffic flows: external → pod →
   308→{at_sentry_container} → {at_bottle_container}
   309→
   310→These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.
   311→
   312→== {opss_sentry_start}
   313→
   314→The
   315→{at_rbm_console}
   316→defines the
   317→{opss_sentry_start},
   318→a
   319→{cmk_external_rule}
   320→which is the precisely ordered sequence of
   321→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   322→needed to start a
   323→{at_sentry_container}
   324→that enforces network policy for a new
   325→{at_bottle_service}.
   326→
   327→=== {mkr_network_create}
   328→
   329→This sequence of
   330→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   331→within the
   332→{opss_sentry_start}
   333→establishes required networks for the
   334→{at_bottle_service}:
   335→
   336→. Remove Existing
   337→{at_transit_network}
   338→* Remove the
   339→{at_transit_network}
   340→named
   341→{at_transit_network_name},
   342→ignoring errors
   343→* This ensures a clean state before creating the new transit network
   344→
   345→. Create New
   346→{at_transit_network}
   347→* Use standard Podman network primitives to create a bridge network named
   348→{at_transit_network_name}
   349→* The IP address range or any other attributes remain consistent with the
   350→{rbrn_moniker}-uplink
   351→definition
   352→* IPv6 remains disabled, and external connectivity is enabled
   353→
   354→. Remove Existing
   355→{at_enclave_network}
   356→* Remove the
   357→{at_enclave_network}
   358→named
   359→{at_enclave_network_name},
   360→ignoring errors
   361→* This ensures a clean state before creating the new enclave network
   362→
   363→. Create New
   364→{at_enclave_network}
   365→* Use standard Podman network primitives to create an isolated bridge network named
   366→{at_enclave_network_name}
   367→* Configure the network with subnet
   368→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
   369→* Disable external connectivity to ensure isolation
   370→* Allow netavark to manage IP address assignment for containers on this network
   371→
   372→After this sequence completes, both networks exist in standard Podman configuration. The
   373→{at_sentry_container}
   374→will connect to both networks, while the
   375→{at_bottle_container}
   376→will connect only to the
   377→{at_enclave_network}.
   378→Before the
   379→{at_bottle_container}
   380→starts, an
   381→{at_ebpf_program}
   382→will be attached to its veth interface to ensure all traffic routes through the
   383→{at_sentry_container}.
   384→
   385→=== {mkr_network_connect}
   386→
   387→This sequence of
   388→{cmk_recipe_line_s}
   389→within the
   390→{opss_sentry_start}
   391→establishes the secondary network interface connected to
   392→{at_enclave_network}:
   393→
   394→. Connect Container to Enclave Network
   395→* Attach the
   396→{at_sentry_container}
   397→to the
   398→{at_enclave_network}
   399→using standard Podman network connect
   400→* Verify successful network attachment
   401→* Validate interface naming sequence - eth0 must be the transit network interface
   402→* The enclave network interface must appear as eth1
   403→
   404→. Verify Network Interface Configuration
   405→* Execute within sentry container context
   406→* {mkc_interface_check}
   407→with interface `eth1` and timeout_s `5`
   408→** Exit on first success
   409→** Fail if timeout reached
   410→
   411→. Configure Sentry IP Address
   412→* Remove the auto-assigned IP address from eth1
   413→* Assign the permanent
   414→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
   415→address to eth1
   416→* This address will serve as the gateway for the
   417→{at_bottle_container}
   418→after eBPF frame rewriting
   419→
   420→After this sequence completes, the
   421→{at_sentry_container}
   422→has connectivity on both networks with the correct IP configuration, ready to receive traffic from the
   423→{at_bottle_container}
   424→once the
   425→{at_ebpf_program}
   426→is attached.
   427→
   428→=== {scr_security_config}
   429→
   430→One unified script executes these
   431→{cmk_script_line_s}
   432→in sequence, halting early if any
   433→{cmk_script_line}
   434→has a nonzero unix status:
   435→
   436→1. {scr_iptables_init} 
   437→2. {scr_port_setup}
   438→3. {scr_access_setup}
   439→4. {scr_dns_step}
   440→
   441→==== Phase 1: {scr_iptables_init} 
   442→
   443→Establishes foundational security state:
   444→
   445→. Base Chain Configuration:
   446→* Flush all rules from filter and nat tables
   447→* Set policy DROP on all base chains (INPUT, FORWARD, OUTPUT)
   448→* Accept loopback interface traffic unconditionally via first rule in INPUT chain
   449→
   450→. State Tracking Setup:
   451→* Insert RELATED,ESTABLISHED tracking as second rule in chains:
   452→** INPUT chain with "-m state --state RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT"
   453→** FORWARD chain with same state match
   454→** OUTPUT chain with same state match
   455→
   456→. RBM Chain Creation:
   457→* Create in filter table with default policy DROP:
   458→** RBM-INGRESS for input control
   459→** RBM-EGRESS for output control
   460→** RBM-FORWARD for forwarding control
   461→* Insert jumps as third rule in respective base chains:
   462→** INPUT jumps to RBM-INGRESS
   463→** OUTPUT jumps to RBM-EGRESS
   464→** FORWARD jumps to RBM-FORWARD
   465→
   466→==== Phase 2: {scr_port_setup}
   467→
   468→If
   469→{rbrn_entry_mode}
   470→is disabled, this sequence is skipped.
   471→If
   472→{rbrn_entry_mode}
   473→is enabled, this sequence configures service port mapping:
   474→
   475→. Socat Proxy Configuration:
   476→* Launch socat proxy process in background:
   477→** Listen on TCP port
   478→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
   479→** Forward connections to
   480→{at_bottle_container}
   481→IP (
   482→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   483→) on port
   484→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   485→** Enable connection forking and address reuse
   486→** Log output to `/var/log/socat-proxy.log`
   487→* Verify socat process started successfully
   488→
   489→. Filter Configuration:
   490→* Add rule to RBM-INGRESS accepting incoming traffic from bridge network:
   491→** Interface eth0
   492→** Protocol TCP
   493→** Destination port
   494→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
   495→* Add rule to RBM-EGRESS accepting outbound connections to bottle:
   496→** Interface eth1
   497→** Protocol TCP
   498→** Destination
   499→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   500→** Destination port
   501→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   502→* Add rule to RBM-INGRESS accepting return traffic from bottle:
   503→** Interface eth1
   504→** Protocol TCP
   505→** Source
   506→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   507→** Source port
   508→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
   509→
   510→==== Phase 3: {scr_access_setup}
   511→
   512→If
   513→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   514→is disabled, this sequence:
   515→
   516→* Verifies DROP policy exists for all filter table chains
   517→* Removes NAT rules except those marked "RBM-PORT-FORWARD"
   518→* Adds explicit DROP rules for non-port traffic:
   519→** RBM-EGRESS: DROP all eth0 output
   520→** RBM-FORWARD: DROP all eth1 forward
   521→
   522→If
   523→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   524→is global or allowlist, this sequence:
   525→
   526→. Network Interface Configuration:
   527→* Enable IPv4 forwarding
   528→* Disable IPv6
   529→* Set rp_filter to strict mode (1) on all interfaces
   530→* Set route_localnet on eth0
   531→
   532→. NAT Configuration:
   533→* Add POSTROUTING on eth0:
   534→** Source from eth1 subnet
   535→** MASQUERADE to eth0 IP
   536→* Preserve rules marked "RBM-PORT-FORWARD"
   537→
   538→. DNS Server Access:
   539→* If
   540→{rbrr_dns_server}
   541→is configured:
   542→** Add rule to RBM-EGRESS accepting traffic to
   543→{rbrr_dns_server}
   544→** Add rule to RBM-FORWARD accepting traffic to
   545→{rbrr_dns_server}
   546→* These rules are independent of
   547→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   548→and
   549→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
   550→
   551→. Filter Configuration:
   552→* If
   553→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode} is global:
   554→** Accept all output on eth0 in RBM-EGRESS
   555→** Accept all forward from eth1 in RBM-FORWARD
   556→* If
   557→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode} is allowlist:
   558→** For each CIDR in
   559→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}:
   560→*** Accept in RBM-EGRESS matching destination
   561→*** Accept in RBM-FORWARD matching destination
   562→* Maintain existing port rules
   563→
   564→==== Phase 4: {scr_dns_step}
   565→
   566→If
   567→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   568→is disabled, the
   569→{at_sentry_container}:
   570→
   571→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1
   572→* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to eth0
   573→* Removes any existing DNS-related NAT rules
   574→
   575→If
   576→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   577→is global or allowlist, the
   578→{at_sentry_container}
   579→executes this sequence:
   580→
   581→. DNS Server Validation:
   582→+
   583→--
   584→* Verify
   585→{rbrr_dns_server}
   586→if specified:
   587→** TCP connect test to port 53 with 5 second timeout
   588→** UDP query for "." record with 6 second timeout
   589→* Fail if either test unsuccessful
   590→--
   591→
   592→. Dnsmasq Configuration:
   593→+
   594→--
   595→* Core Settings:
   596→** Interface binding limited strictly to
   597→{at_enclave_network}
   598→interface eth1
   599→** Use {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
   600→for listen-address
   601→** DNS service only - all DHCP functionality explicitly disabled
   602→** DNS resolution configuration isolated from host system settings
   603→** Clear existing upstream server configuration
   604→** Configure upstream DNS:
   605→*** Use configured upstream server when
   606→{rbrr_dns_server}
   607→specified
   608→*** Otherwise use host resolver via external network interface
   609→
   610→* Cache Settings:
   611→** Cache size: 1000 entries
   612→** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
   613→** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds
   614→
   615→* Query Control:
   616→** When
   617→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   618→is global:
   619→*** Accept all DNS queries from eth1
   620→*** Forward queries unmodified to upstream
   621→*** Log each query with timestamp and resolution
   622→*** Return all responses unmodified
   623→
   624→** When
   625→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   626→is allowlist:
   627→*** Load allowed domains from
   628→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
   629→*** Accept queries only for allowed domains
   630→*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries
   631→*** Configure dnsmasq logging directly with:
   632→**** log-queries=extra
   633→**** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
   634→**** log-dhcp
   635→**** Extra logging options in dnsmasq.conf:
   636→***** log-debug (for detailed query info)
   637→***** log-async=20 (for better performance)
   638→--
   639→
   640→. Filter Rules:
   641→+
   642→--
   643→* Accept DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1:
   644→** UDP port 53
   645→** TCP port 53
   646→* Accept DNS forwarding in RBM-EGRESS to eth0:
   647→** UDP/TCP port 53 to
   648→{rbrr_dns_server}
   649→** Or to host resolver if no server specified
   650→--
   651→
   652→. NAT Configuration:
   653→+
   654→--
   655→* DNAT eth1 DNS to
   656→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}:53
   657→* SNAT outbound queries to eth0 IP
   658→* Preserve existing port forwarding rules
   659→--

   719→== VM Management Operations
   720→
   721→This section defines the operations for managing Podman VM images within the {at_rbm_system}.
   722→
   723→=== {ops_rbv_check}
   724→
   725→The {ops_rbv_check} verifies whether newer VM images are available upstream by comparing SHA digests. This operation uses a temporary {at_stash_machine} with crane installed to query registries without modifying configuration.
   726→
   727→==== {ops_rbv_check} Sequence
   728→
   729→. Create Temporary {at_stash_machine}
   730→* Initialize new Podman machine using name from
   731→{rbrr_stash_machine}
   732→* Start the machine and install crane from
   733→{rbrr_crane_tar_gz}
   734→* Verify crane installation completed successfully
   735→
   736→. Query Upstream Image
   737→* Use crane to query digest of
   738→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin}:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   739→* Store the returned SHA digest for comparison
   740→* Fail if query returns no valid digest
   741→
   742→. Verify Custom FQIN (if applicable)
   743→* If
   744→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}
   745→differs from standard origin:version pattern:
   746→** Query the FQIN directly using crane
   747→** Compare FQIN digest against upstream digest
   748→** Report any discrepancies as warnings
   749→
   750→. Cleanup Resources
   751→* Stop and remove the temporary
   752→{at_stash_machine}
   753→* Ensure no orphaned resources remain
   754→
   755→. Report Findings
   756→* Display current
   757→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   758→value
   759→* Display latest upstream SHA digest
   760→* Indicate required actions:
   761→** If SHA not set: request user to set
   762→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   763→** If SHA differs: notify update available
   764→** If SHA matches: confirm system is current
   765→
   766→=== {ops_rbv_mirror}
   767→
   768→The
   769→{ops_rbv_mirror}
   770→copies the chosen VM image from upstream to the user's GHCR, ensuring consistent deployment across environments.
   771→This operation validates preconditions, performs the copy, and verifies podman can access the mirrored image.
   772→
   773→==== {ops_rbv_mirror} Sequence
   774→
   775→. Validate Preconditions
   776→* If using standard origin (FQIN starts with origin):
   777→** Query current upstream SHA using crane
   778→** Compare with
   779→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   780→if defined
   781→** Fail if SHAs do not match, directing user to run
   782→{ops_rbv_check}
   783→first
   784→
   785→. Generate GHCR Target
   786→* Construct GHCR tag using registry naming pattern
   787→* Format:
   788→ghcr.io/{rbrr_registry_owner}/{rbrr_registry_name}/vmimage:{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   789→
   790→. Check Existing Mirror
   791→* Query GHCR for existing image at target location
   792→* If found and SHA defined:
   793→** Verify existing image SHA matches
   794→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   795→** Exit successfully if match confirmed
   796→** Fail if SHAs differ
   797→
   798→. Copy Image to GHCR
   799→* Use crane to copy from
   800→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}
   801→to GHCR target
   802→* Verify copy completed without errors
   803→* Confirm transferred image integrity
   804→
   805→. Verify Copied Image
   806→* If
   807→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   808→is defined:
   809→** Query digest of newly copied GHCR image
   810→** Compare with expected SHA
   811→** Fail if verification shows mismatch
   812→
   813→. Validate Podman Access
   814→* Initialize test Podman machine using mirrored image
   815→* Capture initialization output through standard scrubbing process
   816→* Extract reported SHA from initialization
   817→* Compare with
   818→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}
   819→if defined
   820→* Remove test machine after validation
   821→
   822→. Report Success
   823→* Confirm mirror operation completed
   824→* Note image available at GHCR location
   825→* Verify podman can successfully use mirrored image
   826→

   858→== {cmk_script} Requirements
   859→
   860→The following requirements apply to all {cmk_script_s} within the {cmk_discipline}:
   861→
   862→=== Environment Variable Availability
   863→
   864→Each
   865→{at_startup_script}
   866→inherits and validates
   867→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
   868→through makefile export directives:
   869→
   870→. At script start, validate existence of all referenced variables using `: ${VARIABLE:?} && echo "VARIABLE = ${VARIABLE}"`
   871→. {cfg_base_regime}, {cfg_station_regime}, and {cfg_nameplate_regime}
   872→export their respective variables
   873→
   874→=== Environment and Structure Requirements
   875→- Each
   876→{cmk_script}
   877→begins with `#!/bin/sh`
   878→- Each
   879→{cmk_script}
   880→enables `set -e`
   881→- Each
   882→{cmk_script}
   883→validates required environment variables using `: ${VARIABLE:?}`
   884→- Each
   885→{cmk_script}
   886→echoes validated variable values
   887→
   888→=== Error Handling Requirements 
   889→- Every command that can fail is followed by `|| exit N` where N is a positive integer
   890→- Error handling applies within if/then/else blocks and loops
   891→- Common commands like echo use exit code 99 for failures
   892→* Exit Code Ranges and Meanings:
   893→** 10: IPTables failures
   894→** 20: Port forwarding configuration failures
   895→** 30: Network access configuration failures 
   896→** 31: Network routing/filtering setup failures
   897→** 32: CIDR rule configuration failures
   898→** 40: DNS server connectivity check failures
   899→** 41: DNS configuration file writing failures
   900→** 42: DNS service startup failures
   901→** 43: DNS rule configuration failures
   902→
   903→=== Control Flow Requirements
   904→- if/then/else statements are permitted for clear flow control
   905→- for/while loops are permitted for iteration
   906→- All commands within control structures maintain error handling
   907→- Break and continue are permitted within loops
   908→- No command substitution without error handling
   909→- No function definitions permitted
   910→- All logic is expressed through direct command sequences
   911→
   912→=== Operation Logging Requirements
   913→- Each echo uses double quotes
   914→- Each log line begins with "RBSpN: " where N is the phase number (1-5)
   915→- Echo statements replace inline comments
   916→- No shell comments (# or multi-line) permitted
   917→- Debug echo statements precede significant operations
   918→
   919→=== Example Conformant Script Structure
   920→
   921→```sh
   922→#!/bin/sh
   923→set -e
   924→
   925→: "${INPUT_DIR:?}"
   926→: "${OUTPUT_DIR:?}"
   927→
   928→echo "XYZp1: Processing files from ${INPUT_DIR} to ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
   929→
   930→if [ ! -d "${INPUT_DIR}" ]; then
   931→    echo "XYZp1: Input directory not found"
   932→    exit 1
   933→fi
   934→
   935→if [ ! -d "${OUTPUT_DIR}" ]; then
   936→    echo "XYZp1: Creating output directory"
   937→    mkdir -p "${OUTPUT_DIR}" || exit 2
   938→fi
   939→
   940→echo "XYZp1: Beginning file conversion"
   941→for src in "${INPUT_DIR}"/*.txt; do
   942→    if [ -f "${src}" ]; then
   943→        echo "XYZp1: Processing ${src}"
   944→        dest="${OUTPUT_DIR}/$(basename "${src}" .txt).upper"
   945→        tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]' < "${src}" > "${dest}" || exit 3
   946→        wc -l "${dest}" > "${dest}.count" || exit 4
   947→    fi
   948→done
   949→
   950→echo "XYZp1: Processing complete"
   951→```

   953→== {cfg_base_regime} Definitions
   954→
   955→This section defines the
   956→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
   957→which come from the
   958→{cfg_base_regime}
   959→that defines the core configuration for the
   960→{at_rbm_system}.
   961→
   962→[[rbrr_dns_server]]
   963→{rbrr_dns_server}:: 
   964→An IPv4 address (e.g., "8.8.8.8") that specifies the upstream DNS resolver for all 
   965→{at_bottle_service_s}.
   966→When configured, the
   967→{at_sentry_container}
   968→forwards DNS queries to this address instead of the host resolver, enabling consistent name resolution across all
   969→{at_bottle_container_s}
   970→while maintaining the established security boundaries.
   971→If not specified, the system uses the host's resolver configuration.
   972→
   973→[[rbrr_nameplate_path]]
   974→{rbrr_nameplate_path}:: 
   975→A relative path (e.g., "recipes/nameplates") that specifies the directory containing all
   976→{at_nameplate}
   977→definitions for this
   978→{at_rbm_system}.
   979→This path is resolved relative to the
   980→{at_rbm_repo}
   981→root, enabling consistent access to configuration files across different installations.
   982→If not specified, the system uses "nameplates" in the repository root.
   983→
   984→[[rbrr_registry_server]]
   985→{rbrr_registry_server}:: 
   986→A fully-qualified domain name and optional port (e.g., "registry.example.com:5000") that specifies the container registry server for all
   987→{at_bottle_service_s}.
   988→The
   989→{at_rbm_system}
   990→retrieves all
   991→{st_published_image_s}
   992→from this registry, ensuring consistent image sourcing across the installation.
   993→If not specified, the system uses the default public container registry.
   994→
   995→[[rbrr_registry_owner]]
   996→{rbrr_registry_owner}::
   997→A GitHub username or organization name that specifies the owner of the container registry namespace where
   998→{st_published_image_s}
   999→are stored.
  1000→
  1001→[[rbrr_registry_name]]
  1002→{rbrr_registry_name}::
  1003→A GitHub repository name that defines the repository within the container registry and forms the complete registry path when combined with
  1004→{rbrr_registry_owner}.
  1005→
  1006→[[rbrr_github_pat_env]]
  1007→{rbrr_github_pat_env}::
  1008→A file path to an environment file containing GitHub credentials for API and
  1009→{at_container_registry}
  1010→authentication with required RBV_USERNAME and RBV_PAT variables.
  1011→
  1012→[[rbrr_build_architectures]]
  1013→{rbrr_build_architectures}::
  1014→A space-separated list of platform identifiers in os/arch format that specifies target architectures for
  1015→{st_container}
  1016→builds.
  1017→
  1018→[[rbrr_history_dir]]
  1019→{rbrr_history_dir}::
  1020→A relative path from the
  1021→{at_rbm_system}
  1022→root that specifies the local directory for storing build artifacts and history from GitHub Actions workflows.
  1023→
  1024→[[rbrr_chosen_podman_version]]
  1025→{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}::
  1026→The user-selected Podman version (e.g., "5.5") that determines which VM image version to use for the RBM system.
  1027→
  1028→[[rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin]]
  1029→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin}::
  1030→The base container registry path (e.g., "quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl") from which Podman VM images are sourced.
  1031→
  1032→[[rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin]]
  1033→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_fqin}::
  1034→The fully qualified image name including registry, repository, and tag that specifies the exact VM image to use.
  1035→
  1036→[[rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha]]
  1037→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_sha}::
  1038→The SHA256 digest of the VM image that ensures consistency and integrity across deployments.
  1039→
  1040→[[rbrr_chosen_identity]]
  1041→{rbrr_chosen_identity}::
  1042→A marker indicating the configuration state or version of the RBM system deployment.
  1043→
  1044→[[rbrr_stash_machine]]
  1045→{rbrr_stash_machine}::
  1046→The name identifier for temporary Podman machines created for registry operations and image verification.
  1047→
  1048→[[rbrr_operational_machine]]
  1049→{rbrr_operational_machine}::
  1050→The name identifier for the primary Podman machine that runs bottle services in production.
  1051→
  1052→[[rbrr_crane_tar_gz]]
  1053→{rbrr_crane_tar_gz}::

  1056→== Architecture Term Definitions
  1057→
  1058→[[bottle_image]]
  1059→{at_bottle_image}::
  1060→A
  1061→{st_image}
  1062→that contains the service functionality intended to run within a
  1063→{at_bottle_container}.
  1064→This image is stored in the
  1065→{at_container_registry}
  1066→and is referenced by
  1067→{rbrn_bottle_vessel} and {rbrn_bottle_consecration}.
  1068→
  1069→[[bottle_service]]
  1070→{at_bottle_service}::
  1071→A complete service instance consisting of one
  1072→{at_sentry_container},
  1073→one
  1074→{at_censer_container},
  1075→and one
  1076→{at_bottle_container}
  1077→working together to provide secure functionality. Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
  1078→
  1079→[[sessile_service]]
  1080→{at_sessile_service}::
  1081→A
  1082→{at_bottle_service}
  1083→that maintains a persistent
  1084→{at_bottle_container}
  1085→running continuously to provide long-term services like development tools or network services.
  1086→These services maintain state and provide stable network ports while enforcing all
  1087→{at_sentry_container}
  1088→security constraints.
  1089→
  1090→[[agile_service]]
  1091→{at_agile_service}::
  1092→A
  1093→{at_bottle_service}
  1094→that creates temporary
  1095→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1096→for single operations, immediately removing them upon completion. This pattern suits toolchain operations that primarily operate through
  1097→{st_volume_mount_s}
  1098→with minimal network requirements.
  1099→
  1100→[[workstation]]
  1101→{at_workstation}::
  1102→The local computing environment where the
  1103→{at_operator}
  1104→runs the
  1105→{at_rbm_system}.
  1106→This environment hosts all
  1107→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1108→and their associated networks while providing isolation from other system resources.
  1109→
  1110→[[enclave_network]]
  1111→{at_enclave_network}::
  1112→An isolated network connecting a
  1113→{at_bottle_container}
  1114→to its
  1115→{at_sentry_container},
  1116→using the
  1117→// TODO involve the new network namespace
  1118→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
  1119→for addressing.
  1120→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only network communication path for the
  1121→{at_bottle_container}.
  1122→
  1123→[[transit_network]]
  1124→{at_transit_network}::
  1125→The network interface that connects a
  1126→{at_sentry_container}
  1127→to the
  1128→{at_workstation_p}
  1129→network resources.
  1130→This network enables controlled external communication while maintaining security boundaries.
  1131→
  1132→[[moniker]]
  1133→{at_moniker}::
  1134→A unique identifier specified by
  1135→{rbrn_moniker}
  1136→that distinguishes each
  1137→{at_bottle_service}
  1138→instance within the
  1139→{at_rbm_system}.
  1140→This identifier forms the basis for container names and network identifiers.
  1141→
  1142→[[nameplate]]
  1143→{at_nameplate}::
  1144→A configuration file stored in
  1145→{rbrr_nameplate_path}
  1146→that defines all aspects of a
  1147→{at_bottle_service},
  1148→including its images, network settings, and security policies.
  1149→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the
  1150→{at_rbm_system}
  1151→can instantiate.
  1152→
  1153→[[transit_network_name]]
  1154→{at_transit_network_name}::
  1155→The unique network name formed by appending `-uplink` to the service's
  1156→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1157→The
  1158→{at_rbm_system}
  1159→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1160→`${rbrn_moniker}-uplink`
  1161→to identify the external-facing network for each
  1162→{at_sentry_container}
  1163→within the container runtime environment.
  1164→
  1165→[[enclave_network_name]]
  1166→{at_enclave_network_name}::
  1167→The unique network name formed by appending `-enclave` to the service's
  1168→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1169→The
  1170→{at_rbm_system}
  1171→uses this consistent naming pattern `${rbrn_moniker}-enclave` to identify the isolated internal network connecting each
  1172→{at_bottle_container}
  1173→to its
  1174→{at_sentry_container}.
  1175→
  1176→[[enclave_namespace_name]]
  1177→{at_enclave_namespace_name}::
  1178→The
  1179→// TODO enclave, transit, network, but then namespace: this all needs a term rebalance... but later
  1180→{at_bottle_service}
  1181→unique namespace name formed by appending `-namespace` to the service's
  1182→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1183→// TODO this definition is not precise, but I'm not currently smart enough to repair it
  1184→The
  1185→{at_rbm_system}
  1186→uses this consistent naming pattern `${rbrn_moniker}-namespace` to identify the isolated internal network namespace connecting each
  1187→{at_bottle_container}
  1188→to its
  1189→{at_sentry_container}.
  1190→
  1191→[[sentry_image]]
  1192→{at_sentry_image}::
  1193→A
  1194→{st_image}
  1195→that contains the security enforcement functionality for a
  1196→{at_sentry_container}.
  1197→This image is stored in the
  1198→{at_container_registry}
  1199→and is referenced by
  1200→{rbrn_sentry_vessel} and {rbrn_sentry_consecration}.
  1201→
  1202→[[sentry_container]]
  1203→{at_sentry_container}::
  1204→A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a
  1205→{at_bottle_service}.
  1206→It connects to both
  1207→{at_transit_network} and {at_enclave_network},
  1208→controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
  1209→
  1210→[[censer_container]]
  1211→{at_censer_container}::
  1212→A privileged container using a minimal image that establishes and configures the network namespace before the
  1213→{at_bottle_container}
  1214→starts.
  1215→It sets up routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
  1216→{at_sentry_container},
  1217→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
  1218→{at_bottle_container}
  1219→via `--net=container:censer`.
  1220→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet.
  1221→
  1222→[[bottle_container]]
  1223→{at_bottle_container}::
  1224→A container that runs the actual service functionality, inheriting its network namespace from the
  1225→{at_censer_container}.
  1226→It is connected only to the
  1227→{at_enclave_network}
  1228→and configured with specific
  1229→{st_volume_mount_s}.
  1230→All external communication flows through its associated
  1231→{at_sentry_container}.
  1232→
  1233→[[bottle_container_name]]
  1234→{at_bottle_container_name}::
  1235→The 
  1236→{at_bottle_container}
  1237→formed by appending `-bottle` to the service's
  1238→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1239→The
  1240→{at_rbm_system}
  1241→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1242→`${rbrn_moniker}-bottle`
  1243→to identify all
  1244→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1245→within the container runtime environment, enabling reliable service management and status tracking.
  1246→
  1247→[[sentry_container_name]]
  1248→{at_sentry_container_name}::
  1249→The unique container name formed by appending `-sentry` to the service's
  1250→{rbrn_moniker}.
  1251→The
  1252→{at_rbm_system}
  1253→uses this consistent naming pattern
  1254→`${rbrn_moniker}-sentry`
  1255→to identify all
  1256→{at_sentry_container_s}
  1257→within the container runtime environment, enabling reliable security policy enforcement and network management.
  1258→
  1259→[[rbm_console]]
  1260→{at_rbm_console}::
  1261→The primary
  1262→{cmk_makefile}
  1263→that implements the
  1264→{at_rbm_system_p}
  1265→operational rules. It provides
  1266→{cmk_external_rule_s} and {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1267→for managing
  1268→{at_bottle_service_s}
  1269→throughout their lifecycle.
  1270→
  1271→[[rbm_config_makefile]]
  1272→{at_rbm_config_makefile}::
  1273→A
  1274→{cmk_makefile}
  1275→that manages configuration settings for the
  1276→{at_rbm_system},
  1277→including
  1278→{at_rbm_secret_s}
  1279→and environment-specific parameters.
  1280→This file typically remains outside version control to protect sensitive data.
  1281→
  1282→[[rbm_system]]
  1283→{at_rbm_system}::
  1284→The complete Recipe Bottle Makefile system, consisting of the
  1285→{at_rbm_console}, {at_rbm_config_makefile}, {at_nameplate_s},
  1286→and associated tools.
  1287→This system enables secure deployment and management of containerized services.
  1288→
  1289→[[rbm_secret]]
  1290→{at_rbm_secret}::
  1291→A sensitive configuration value needed by the
  1292→{at_rbm_system},
  1293→such as
  1294→registry credentials.
  1295→These secrets are managed through the
  1296→{at_rbm_config_makefile}
  1297→and kept separate from version control.
  1298→
  1299→[[rbm_repo]]
  1300→{at_rbm_repo}::
  1301→The version control repository containing the
  1302→{at_rbm_system}
  1303→implementation, including the
  1304→{at_rbm_console}, {at_nameplate_s},
  1305→and documentation.
  1306→This repository serves as the authoritative source for system configuration.
  1307→
  1308→[[user_repo]]
  1309→{at_user_repo}::
  1310→A version control repository owned by an
  1311→{at_operator}.
  1312→The
  1313→{at_operator}
  1314→chooses how to instantiate the
  1315→{at_rbm_system}
  1316→there, though a git subtree is a good choice.
  1317→The
  1318→{at_operator}
  1319→configures the
  1320→{at_rbm_system}
  1321→by committing a
  1322→{cfg_base_regime}
  1323→makefile and then creates a set of
  1324→{at_nameplate_s}
  1325→compliant with the
  1326→{cfg_nameplate_regime}
  1327→to define
  1328→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1329→
  1330→[[container_registry]]
  1331→{at_container_registry}:: 
  1332→A service that stores and provides access to 
  1333→{st_published_image_s}.
  1334→
  1335→[[startup_script]]
  1336→{at_startup_script}::
  1337→A
  1338→{cmk_script}
  1339→that performs initialization and configuration tasks when starting a
  1340→{at_bottle_service}.
  1341→These scripts follow strict requirements for execution environment, logging, and error handling.
  1342→
  1343→[[operator]]
  1344→{at_operator}::
  1345→An individual who maintains a local installation of the
  1346→{at_rbm_system}
  1347→on their
  1348→{at_workstation}
  1349→and holds RBRA Files that enable assumption of the system's defined roles.
  1350→
  1351→[[consumer]]
  1352→{rbtr_consumer}::
  1353→The role an
  1354→{at_operator}
  1355→assumes to run containerized services securely on their
  1356→{at_workstation}.
  1357→{rbtr_consumer_s}
  1358→control service configuration through
  1359→{at_nameplate_s}
  1360→and interact with services through controlled interfaces, distinct from roles that build and deploy the system.
  1361→
  1362→[[at_ebpf_program]]
  1363→{at_ebpf_program}::
  1364→An eBPF TC (Traffic Control) filter program attached to the
  1365→{at_bottle_container_p}
  1366→veth interface that intercepts and rewrites Ethernet frames at the packet level.
  1367→This program replaces the default gateway IP address assigned by netavark with the
  1368→{at_sentry_container_p}
  1369→IP address in all outbound frames, ensuring all
  1370→{at_bottle_container}
  1371→network traffic routes through its associated
  1372→{at_sentry_container}
  1373→without requiring network namespace manipulation or special container privileges.
  1374→
  1375→[[base_regime]]
  1376→{cfg_base_regime}::
  1377→A
  1378→{crg_regime}
  1379→that defines and enforces core system configuration through environment variables with the RBB_*
  1380→{crg_prefix}.
  1381→The
  1382→{at_rbm_repo}
  1383→contains exactly one Base Config makefile that exports these variables, establishing foundational configuration for a single
  1384→{at_rbm_system}
  1385→installation.
  1386→
  1387→[[station_regime]]
  1388→{cfg_station_regime}::
  1389→A
  1390→{crg_regime}
  1391→that defines and enforces deployment-specific configuration through environment variables with the RBS_*
  1392→{crg_prefix}.
  1393→Each
  1394→{at_operator}
  1395→maintains their own Station Config makefile outside version control, containing sensitive data like authentication tokens required for their specific installation.
  1396→
  1397→[[nameplate_regime]]
  1398→{cfg_nameplate_regime}::
  1399→A
  1400→{crg_regime}
  1401→that defines and enforces service configuration through environment variables with the RBRN_*
  1402→{crg_prefix}.
  1403→Each
  1404→{at_bottle_service}
  1405→has exactly one
  1406→{at_nameplate}
  1407→makefile that exports these variables, establishing that service's precise behavior and constraints.
  1408→
  1409→[[regime_variable]]
  1410→{cfg_regime_variable}::
  1411→A
  1412→{crg_variable}
  1413→exported by one of the three configuration regime makefiles, following that regime's
  1414→{crg_prefix}
  1415→(RBB_*, RBS_*, or RBRN_*).
  1416→Each
  1417→{at_startup_script}
  1418→validates required
  1419→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1420→using the ': ${VARIABLE:?}' pattern before use.
  1421→
  1422→[[stash_machine]]
  1423→{at_stash_machine}::
  1424→A temporary Podman virtual machine created with crane installed for performing registry queries and image operations.
  1425→These machines are removed after completing their specific tasks.
  1426→
  1427→[[operational_machine]]
  1428→{at_operational_machine}::
  1429→The persistent Podman virtual machine that hosts all bottle services and their associated containers in the RBM system.
  1430→
  1431→== {cfg_station_regime} Definitions
  1432→
  1433→This section defines the
  1434→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1435→which come from the
  1436→{cfg_station_regime}

  1439→
  1440→This
  1441→{crg_regime}

  1446→
  1447→This section defines the
  1448→{cfg_regime_variable_s}
  1449→which come from the

  1451→Each
  1452→{at_bottle_service}
  1453→has exactly one
  1454→{at_nameplate}
  1455→makefile that exports these variables, establishing that service's precise behavior and constraints.
  1456→
  1457→[[rbrn_moniker]]
  1458→{rbrn_moniker}::
  1459→A unique identifier that distinguishes this
  1460→{at_bottle_service}
  1461→from others in the
  1462→{at_rbm_system}.
  1463→
  1464→[[rbrn_description]]
  1465→{rbrn_description}::
  1466→A human-readable explanation of the
  1467→{at_bottle_service_p}
  1468→purpose and function.
  1469→
  1470→[[rbrn_sentry_moniker]]
  1471→{rbrn_sentry_moniker}::
  1472→The image moniker identifier for the
  1473→{at_sentry_image}
  1474→in the
  1475→{at_container_registry}.
  1476→
  1477→[[rbrn_bottle_moniker]]
  1478→{rbrn_bottle_moniker}::
  1479→The image moniker identifier for the
  1480→{at_bottle_image}
  1481→in the
  1482→{at_container_registry}.
  1483→
  1484→
  1485→[[rbrn_entry_mode]]
  1486→{rbrn_entry_mode}::
  1487→The mode controlling whether the
  1488→{at_bottle_service}
  1489→exposes network ports through the
  1490→{at_sentry_container}.
  1491→When disabled, no entry ports are configured.
  1492→When enabled, entry ports are mapped through the
  1493→{at_sentry_container}.
  1494→
  1495→[[rbrn_entry_port_workstation]]
  1496→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}::
  1497→The network port number exposed on the
  1498→{at_transit_network}
  1499→when port service is enabled.
  1500→
  1501→[[rbrn_entry_port_enclave]]
  1502→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}::
  1503→The network port number used for communication between
  1504→{at_sentry_container}
  1505→and
  1506→{at_bottle_container}
  1507→across the
  1508→{at_enclave_network}.
  1509→
  1510→[[rbrn_uplink_dns_mode]]
  1511→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}::
  1512→The DNS resolution mode for bottle-initiated requests through the
  1513→{at_sentry_container}.
  1514→When disabled, the
  1515→{at_sentry_container} blocks all DNS queries from the
  1516→{at_bottle_container}.
  1517→When global, the
  1518→{at_sentry_container}
  1519→forwards all DNS queries unmodified to upstream.
  1520→When allowlist, the
  1521→{at_sentry_container}
  1522→allows resolution only for domains listed in
  1523→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
  1524→and returns NXDOMAIN for all other queries.
  1525→
  1526→[[rbrn_uplink_access_mode]]
  1527→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}::
  1528→The IP access mode for bottle-initiated connections through the
  1529→{at_transit_network}.
  1530→When disabled, all non-port traffic is blocked.
  1531→When global, unrestricted IP access is allowed.
  1532→When allowlist, only traffic to CIDRs listed in
  1533→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
  1534→is permitted.
  1535→
  1536→[[rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs]]
  1537→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}::
  1538→A space-separated list of CIDR ranges that define allowed outbound traffic through the
  1539→{at_transit_network}.
  1540→
  1541→[[rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains]]
  1542→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}::
  1543→A space-separated list of domain names that are allowed for DNS resolution through the
  1544→{at_sentry_container}.
  1545→
  1546→[[rbrn_enclave_netmask]]
  1547→{rbrn_enclave_netmask}::
  1548→The network mask width defining the size of address space available for the
  1549→{at_enclave_network}.
  1550→
  1551→[[rbrn_enclave_base_ip]]
  1552→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}::
  1553→The base IPv4 address that establishes the starting point of the
  1554→{at_enclave_network}
  1555→range.
  1556→
  1557→[[rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip]]
  1558→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}::
  1559→The permanent gateway IP address (typically .0.1) assigned to the
  1560→{at_sentry_container}
  1561→on the
  1562→{at_enclave_network}
  1563→after removing the initial auto-assigned address. This address serves as the gateway for all
  1564→{at_bottle_container_s}
  1565→on the
  1566→{at_enclave_network}.
  1567→
  1568→[[rbrn_volume_mounts]]
  1569→{rbrn_volume_mounts}::
  1570→A space-separated list of Podman volume mount arguments that define filesystem access for the
  1571→{at_bottle_container}.

  1573→== Console Makefile Elements
  1574→
  1575→[[cmk_console_discipline]]
  1576→{cmk_discipline}::
  1577→A systematic approach to automation that defines patterns for creating reliable, maintainable build systems through careful rule organization, strict error handling, and standardized operational sequencing. This discipline establishes conventions for multi-pass construction, naming patterns, and the relationships between makefiles, scripts, and configuration files.
  1578→
  1579→[[cmk_makefile]]
  1580→{cmk_makefile}::
  1581→A specific makefile implementation that follows the
  1582→{cmk_discipline},
  1583→expressing concrete automation through properly structured
  1584→{cmk_external_rule_s}, {cmk_internal_rule_s}, and {cmk_script_s}
  1585→while maintaining prescribed error handling and operational patterns.
  1586→
  1587→[[cmk_clause]]
  1588→{cmk_clause}::
  1589→A standardized sequence of commands or expressions within a
  1590→{cmk_makefile}
  1591→that provides consistent behavior across different rules and contexts while maintaining
  1592→{cmk_discipline}
  1593→patterns.
  1594→These clauses can be invoked directly, used with $(call), or incorporated into larger recipes.
  1595→
  1596→[[cmk_recipe_line]]
  1597→{cmk_recipe_line}::
  1598→A single command line within a
  1599→{cmk_external_rule} or {cmk_internal_rule}
  1600→that follows
  1601→{cmk_discipline}
  1602→conventions, expressing one concrete bash action.
  1603→
  1604→[[cmk_probe_line]]
  1605→{cmk_probe_line}::
  1606→A single makefile recipe line that executes a precise command within a container context.
  1607→Recipe lines form the verification backbone of the
  1608→{cmk_discipline},
  1609→ensuring proper state progression through operational sequences.
  1610→
  1611→[[cmk_internal_rule]]
  1612→{cmk_internal_rule}::
  1613→A makefile rule that coordinates implementation details within the
  1614→{cmk_discipline},
  1615→called by other
  1616→{cmk_external_rule_s} or {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1617→to perform specific operational sequences.
  1618→
  1619→[[cmk_external_rule]]
  1620→{cmk_external_rule}::
  1621→A makefile rule that provides primary interface points for
  1622→{at_operator}
  1623→use, typically invoked by shell scripts or direct user commands to initiate major system operations.
  1624→
  1625→[[cmk_script]]
  1626→{cmk_script}::
  1627→A bash script that exists as an integral part of a
  1628→{cmk_discipline}
  1629→installation, executed under control of
  1630→{cmk_external_rule_s} or {cmk_internal_rule_s}
  1631→and sharing the makefile's operational context and constraints.
  1632→
  1633→[[cmk_script_line]]
  1634→{cmk_script_line}::
  1635→One or more command lines within a
  1636→{cmk_script}
  1637→that achieve a particular action in the script's operational sequence, following
  1638→{cmk_discipline}
  1639→conventions for error handling and status reporting.
  1640→Any failure in a script line results in a non-zero exit status.
  1641→
